_meta.purpose: Cluster-detector registry for consolidate-nudge.sh. A SessionStart sibling to reflect-nudge.sh: reflect-nudge watches reflection CADENCE (how many new beats since the last reflection); this watches cluster SIZE (how many beats pile up under one topic). When a single topic token appears in >= config.threshold session_/reflection_ beats, the hook emits an advisory additionalContext nudging the human to run /consolidate <topic>. Advisory only, human-gated, never blocks - it just surfaces the biggest redundant cluster.
_meta.count_rule: For each beat file: strip .md, strip a leading session_/reflection_ prefix, strip the YYYY-MM-DD date, split the remainder on [_-]+, lowercase, drop tokens shorter than min_token_len, pure-digit tokens, and stopwords. Dedupe tokens within a single file so one file can never inflate a count. A cluster's count is the number of FILES containing that token. Fire when the largest cluster count >= threshold.
_meta.exclusion_rule: MEMORY.md and MEMORY-archive.md are never counted. A beat counts toward a cluster ONLY if its frontmatter `type` is in config.consolidatable_types (default just `project`). This is AUTHORITATIVE and filename-agnostic on purpose: standing beats (type decision/feedback/reference/user) are frequently saved with a session_ FILENAME but a standing TYPE - e.g. session_2026-05-29_tilt-lab-design-direction.md is type: decision. Excluding by filename prefix alone (like compact-memory.py's STANDING tuple) would wrongly sweep those long-lived decision/feedback beats into a merge = data loss. So we read frontmatter type, not the filename. A file with no parseable type is excluded (safe under-count). Example: of the 49 tilt-token files, only the 42 type:project ones count; the 4 decision + 3 feedback (6 of which have session_ filenames) are excluded.
_meta.cooldown_rule: When the hook fires it writes the epoch to config.cooldown_state_file; it stays silent while that file is younger than config.cooldown_seconds, so the same standing cluster is not re-nagged every single session. Override seconds with env CONSOLIDATE_COOLDOWN and the state-file path with env CONSOLIDATE_COOLDOWN_FILE (used by the test harness). CONSOLIDATE_COOLDOWN=0 disables the cooldown (always eligible).
_meta.override_rule: Env overrides for tests: CONSOLIDATE_MEMORY_DIR (point at a temp beats dir; takes precedence over SESSION_CWD), CONSOLIDATE_THRESHOLD (integer), CONSOLIDATE_COOLDOWN (seconds), CONSOLIDATE_COOLDOWN_FILE (path). Tune the stopwords/threshold/cooldown here - this file is the single source of truth.
